title: libpod: don't generate errors for createTimer etc.
body:
Caller will just log the error which is confusing for the user. This matches the linux non-systemd behaviour.

It would be nice to have timer support for healthcheck on FreeBSD but the only pre-installed timer option is at which probably isn't a good fit here.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: /hold Can you force push, something is very wrong with CI?! All cirrus jobs are missing from the github job list. cc @cevich

> change LGTM but I assume this also runs into #19326 in case you have interest in fixing that.

I have seen the same issue with the same partial solution (manually running podman healthcheck). It would be nice to have a healthcheck solution for non-systemd setups. Perhaps conmon or podman service could run the timers or maybe I can find a simple open-source second-resolution alternative to ```at```.

> All cirrus jobs are missing from the github job list. cc @cevich

I've seen this happen before.  Force-pushing at a future time usually fixes it.  I'm not certain yet if this is a one-off or systemic, as I sit near the top of my post-PTO e-mail pile.  I'll keep my eyes out though.

This is happening all over the place.
/hold cancel
Saving the planet since the appropriate testing for BSD was done.
